questions.question_text: Simplify: $$ \frac{16}{21} \div \left( \frac{4}{5} \times \frac{10}{12} + \frac{7}{4} \right) \div \frac{8}{14} \text{ of } \frac{14}{8} $$
In quadrilateral PQRS, $\angle P = 2\angle Q$, $\angle Q = \frac{\angle R}{4}$, $\angle R = \angle S$. Find the value of $\angle S$.
The value of $ \left( 2 + \frac{3}{2} \right)^2 - \left[ 4 - \left( \frac{6}{3} \right)^3 \right] \div \frac{1}{2} $ is
A car travels 280 km in 6.5 hours, partly at 40 km/h and partly at 160 km/h. Find the distance travelled at 160 km/h.

Two banks X and B offered loans at 3.5% and 8.5% per annum, respectively. Ashish borrows an amount of ₹3,40,000 from each bank. Find the positive difference between the amounts of simple interest (in ₹) paid to the two banks by Ashish after 2 years.

Three positive numbers are in proportion such that the ratio of the first number to the second number is the same as the ratio of the second number to the third number. If the first number is 16 and the second number is 24, find the value of the third number.

The price of an article is increased by 20%. In the next step, it is decreased by x% of the new price. Finally, the price is increased by 10% of the original price. If the final price becomes exactly equal to the original price, the value of x is:
A four-digit number 3P2Q is divisible by both 2 and 3. Find the smallest value of P + Q.
If the mean proportional between x and 36 is 12, find x.
The price of an article is first increased by 20% and then decreased by 20%. If the original price of the article is ₹500, what is the net percentage change in the price?

A man bought an article and sold it at a profit of 15%. If he had bought it at 25% less cost price and sold it for ₹494 less than the original selling price, he would have made a profit of 30%. Find the cost price of the article.
A bus travels 90 km at 45 km/h, 120 km at 60 km/h, and 150 km at 75 km/h. What is the average speed of the bus for the entire journey?

Pipes A and B can fill a tank in 12 hours and 15 hours, respectively. A third pipe C can empty the full tank in 20 hours. If A and B are opened together for 2 hours and then C is also opened, what is the total time taken to fill the tank?
Arun sold an article for ₹1,786.40 and incurred a loss of 12%. At what price should he sell the article to earn a profit of 14%?

Two taps can fill a cistern in 4 hours and 8 hours, respectively. A third tap can empty it in 8 hours. How long (in hours) will it take to fill one-fourth of the empty cistern if all the taps are opened together?
The cost of 5 chairs and 3 tables is ₹12,500, while the cost of 3 chairs and 5 tables is ₹15,900. Find the cost of 2 chairs and 2 tables.
Sixteen identical solid cubes, each of side 2 cm, are melted and recast into a cuboid whose length is twice its breadth and height equals its breadth. Find the volume of the cuboid.
The combined present age of P and Q is 46 years. 2 years ago from now, P was twice as old Q. What is the present age of P?

The sides of two square gardens are 18 m and 25 m, respectively. A third square garden is to be constructed such that the length of its side is the mean proportional between the sides of the given two gardens. What will be the length of the side of the new square garden?
A specialized printing machine can print 2400 pages in 1 hour and 20 minutes. At this steady ratio, how many pages can the machine print in 45 minutes?
Three numbers are in the ratio $9:7:5$, and their LCM is 2205. Their HCF is:
The sum of two positive numbers is 32 and their product is 220. The positive difference between them is:
What sum (in ₹) will earn a simple interest of ₹1,440 in 2 years at 16% per year rate of interest?

If the mean of the following data is 40, find the missing frequency.
  $$ \begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|} \hline x & 10 & 20 & 30 & 40 & 60 \\ \hline \text{Frequency} & 2 & 3 & 4 & ? & 4 \\ \hline \end{array} $$

A person standing on level ground looks up at the top of a vertical tower. The angle of elevation formed at the observer's eye is $60^\circ$. If the tower is 18 m high, determine the horizontal distance (in meters) between the person and the base of the tower.

Find the sum of the surface areas of three spheres of radii 2 cm, 3 cm and 6 cm.
  ($\text{Take } \pi = \frac{22}{7}$)

A shopkeeper offers the following schemes on toys at the same marked price.
  (A) Successive discounts of 24% and 33% on any number of toys bought.
  (B) Successive discount of 38%, 37% and 23% on any number of toys bought.
  (C) 24% discount on first 3 toys and 15% discount on each toy thereon.
  (D) 2 toys free of cost on buying 5 toys.
  Which of the above schemes is the least beneficial to her?
An article is marked at ₹3,000. The shopkeeper offers successive discounts of 10% and 20%. What is the final selling price of the article?

Find the mode for the following distribution.
  $$ \begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|} \hline \text{CL} & 30-45 & 45-50 & 50-55 & 55-60 & 60-65 & 65-70 & 70-75 \\ \hline \text{Frequency} & 4 & 4 & 13 & 5 & 6 & 5 & 1 \\ \hline \end{array} $$
Which of the following swimmers won gold in the 200 m breaststroke at the 2023 World Aquatics Championships, setting a championship record and an American record?

options.text: Andaman & Nicobar Islands, Lakshadweep, Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu

questions.question_text: The President of India can make regulations for peace, progress, and good governance of which Union Territories without Legislative Assemblies?
Children of Indian citizens born abroad become Indian citizens by descent only if the birth is registered at an Indian consulate within what time period?
A user wants to quickly pause work and resume in few minutes without waiting for the computer to reboot. The best option is:

options.text: A rural uprising against exploitative landlords
A protest against British education policy

questions.question_text: Which of the following portrays the movement led by Baba Ramchandra?

options.text: Increased nitrate levels causing algal blooms
Evaporation of pesticides from water bodies into the air
Rapid sedimentation of silt at the river bottom
Rising concentration of DDT from water to algae to fish to birds

options.text: Organic farming does not use synthetic chemicals.
Organic farming is beneficial for soil biota.
Organic farming relies heavily on high technology.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements is INCORRECT regarding organic farming?
Which of the following strategies is crucial for fostering infrastructure development in developing countries?
Under whose chairmanship was a 12-member drafting committee recently constituted to frame a law under the High Seas Treaty 'BBNJ'?
What language is known for its unique script called "Gurmukhi"?
Article 371A of the Constitution of India states 'privilege of special status was also accorded to the North-Eastern State of Nagaland'. This represents which federalism?
The Cholas came to power after overthrowing the authority of which of the following dynasties in South India?

options.text: Refreshes the current window or page

questions.question_text: Which action does pressing the F5 key usually perform in Windows?
On 3 November 2025, which state government started the Chief Minister Jibon Prerana Scheme?
Which award is presented to the women's singles champion at the Wimbledon Tennis Championships?
Which substance would you use to test if lemon juice contains acid?
In Harappan Civilization, at Dholavira, _______ was/were used to build massive fortifications.

According to the Taurus Capital report on India's Green Building Market, India's green building sector is projected to grow at a CAGR of 10.5% and reach an estimated size of $85 billion by which year?
Which organisation's leaders gathered in Tianjin for a summit in late August-early September, 2025?
Which of these is NOT an example of fundamental quantity?
Which of the following is the largest multipurpose river valley project in India?
RAM and ROM belong to which broader category?
You can often hear a person speaking from behind a tall fence even if you cannot see them. Which property of sound waves explains this phenomenon?
Which of the following rivers is primarily responsible for the formation of badland topography in India?
Which Indian state has the Konkan Coast along its western shoreline?

In India, the concept of judicial activism, as reflected through innovative interpretations of the Constitution and expansion of the scope of Judicial Review, was introduced during which of the following periods?
The Pallava temples at Mahabalipuram are made mainly from:
Which of the following geographical factors most directly influences the location and growth of human settlements?
Which of the following is a non-point source of water pollution?
Who led the Portuguese expedition that first landed in Calicut, India, in 1498?

options.text: Atomic Energy Act, 1962 & Energy Conservation Act, 2001

questions.question_text: Which Acts are being amended to allow private sector participation in India's nuclear energy sector?
After defeating the Shakas and annexing their territories in western India, which Gupta ruler assumed the imperial title "Vikramaditya" to mark his supremacy?

options.text: Promises and undertakings made by Ministers in Parliament
Monitoring of government expenditure in Parliament

questions.question_text: The Committee on Government Assurances primarily deals with _____.
World Toilet Day was observed on which date in 2025?
Which Article of the Indian Constitution directs the State to protect and improve the environment?

Which of the following countries participated in the $3^\text{rd}$ edition of the Joint Special Forces Exercise 'CYCLONE' at Mahajan Field Firing Ranges in Rajasthan from $10^\text{th}$ to $23^\text{rd}$ February 2025?

Move the program to another drive
Force the process to terminate and release its resources

questions.question_text: When a program in Windows appears as "Not Responding" and the user ends it using Task Manager, which action is effectively being requested from the operating system?
When moving down a group in the Modern Periodic Table, what happens to the number of valence electrons?
In a row of 38 people facing the north, Keerthi is $13^\text{th}$ from the right end. If Tanvi sits $7^\text{th}$ to the left of Keerthi, what is Tanvi's position from the left end of the row?

Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?
  (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter cluster.)

Which letter cluster will replace the question mark (?) to complete the given series?
  CDA, DFU, EKR, ?, GLL

Refer to the following letter series and answer the question that follows.
  (Left) H U N V Y M L N D M Q B E M N Z W M J I N F Q N (Right)
  How many letters are there in the English alphabetical series between the letter which is fifth from the right end and eighth from the left end of the series?

Seven boxes G, H, I, J, Q, R and S, are kept one over the other, but not necessarily in the same order.
  Only three boxes are kept between I and R. Only J is kept above S. Only three boxes are kept below I. H is kept at some place below G but at some place above Q.
  Which box is kept third above Q?

Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete?
  RHE 53, WMC 61, BRA 69, GWY 77, ?

What should come in place of '?' in the given series?
  135 140 160 200 245 ?

Abhinav starts from Point A and drives 16 km towards the west. He then takes a right turn, drives 9 km, turns right and drives 7 km. He then takes a left turn and drives 14 km. He takes a final right turn, drives 3 km and stops at Point P. How far (shortest distance) and in which direction does Abhinav drive in order to reach Point A again?
  (All turns are 90-degree turns only unless specified.)
Each of the digits in the number 7632198 are arranged in ascending order from left to right. How many (such) of how many digits will remain unchanged as compared to that in the original number?

Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?
  (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster pairs.)

Based on the alphabetical order, three of the following four are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?
  (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter cluster.)

What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order?
  PCO QGL RKI SOF ?

In a certain code language,
  A @ B means 'A is the sister of B'.
  A # B means 'A is the daughter of B'.
  A % B means 'A is the wife of B'.
  A + B means 'A is the husband of B'.
  Based on the above, how is V related to R if V - D + Q % T + R'?
DI 12 is related to JE 3 in a certain way. In the same way, EW 11 is related to KS 2. To which of the following is KN 19 related, following the same logic?

Refer to the given number series and answer the question that follows. Counting to be done from left to right only. All numbers are single-digit numbers only.
  (Left) 8 4 7 3 2 1 9 1 8 4 8 7 5 2 1 4 6 3 2 9 8 7 5 4 1 5 2 6 9 8 7 5 6 3 2 1 5 4 7 8 5 1 2 5 9 6 3 2 5 7 8 1 5 4 2 5 8 (Right)
  How many such even digits are there, each of which is immediately preceded by a perfect square and immediately followed by an odd digit? (NOTE: 1 is also a perfect square.)

In certain code language, 'JACK' is coded as '9621' and 'CALK' is coded as '6231'.
  What is the code for 'L' in the given code language?

Refer to the following series and answer the question. (Counting to be done from left to right only.) (All numbers are single-digit numbers only.)
  (Left) 8 5 3 6 9 2 4 2 8 9 7 4 7 7 5 8 2 3 (Right)
  How many such odd digits are there, each of which is immediately preceded by an even digit and also immediately followed by an odd digit?

What should come in place of '?' in the given series?
  284 225 175 130 90 55 ?

Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s).
  Statements:
  (I) Some trees are trees.
  (II) All chairs are trees.
  Conclusions:
  (I) Some trees are chairs.
  (II) All trees are chairs.

Seven people F, G, H, L, M, O and Q are sitting in a row facing north. Only Q sits to the left of H. Only four people sit between Q and G. Only F sits between L and M, and L is not an immediate neighbor of G. How many people sit between O and H?

In the following number-pairs, the second number is obtained by applying certain mathematical operations to the first number. Select the set in which the numbers are NOT related in the same way as the numbers in the given number-pairs.
  (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. E.g., 13 can be 13, not 1 and 3. Operations such as subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)
  78 - 88
  32 - 42
WTQT is related to VSPS in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, VWYW is related to YVSV. To which of the following is OLIL related, following the same logic?

Six boxes E,F,G,H,I and B are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only three boxes are kept above B. Only one box is kept between H and I. Only three boxes are kept between H and I. I is kept at some place above F. B is kept immediately below I. B is kept at one of the places below G. How many boxes are kept between G and H?
If 1 is added to each odd digit and 1 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 7254136, how many digits will appear more than once in the new number thus formed?

In a certain code language, 'GLOW' is coded as '3187' and 'WOLF' is coded as '1273'.
  What is the code for 'G' in the given code language?
LI 32 is related to JH 6 in a certain way. In the same way, CR 28 is related to AN 4. To which of the following is GN 14 related, following the same logic?

If 1 is added to each odd digit and 2 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 825487, then what will be the sum of the first digit from the left and the second digit from the right in the new number thus formed?

Seven people X, Y, Z, A, B, C and D are sitting around a circular table facing towards the centre of the table. D sits third to the right of Y. B is sitting immediately between X and C. A sits second to the right of X. What is the position of A with respect to Z?

In a certain code language,
  A @ B means 'A is the mother of B'.
  A $ \$$ B means 'A is the daughter of B'.
  A % B means 'A is the wife of B'.
  A + B means 'A is the husband of B'.
  How is B related to R if 'C ? O : V @ E + R'?

Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s).
  Statements:
  (I) Some trees are trees.
  (II) All chairs are trees.
  Conclusions:
  (I) Some trees are chairs.
  (II) All trees are chairs.
